commands.review_summary:
The PR closes the shared Codex app-server client after one-shot runs, threads cleanup flags through CLI/gateway paths, and adds broader Slack context, execution-hook, model-routing, supervisor, protocol, and test changes.

PR surface: Source +806, Tests +376, Docs +107, Other +256. Total +1545 across 38 files.

Reproducibility: yes. for the review blockers: the PR head source directly contains the token-shaped value, private endpoint/path, Slack Agent Hub fetch, and hard-coded model filter. I did not independently run the original process-leak scenario, but the contributor supplied live gateway/process-check proof for the teardown behavior.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] The security-sensitive hook files and hard-coded routing changes are concrete repair targets, but maintainers still need to decide whether any split-out Agent Hub or Slack behavior belongs in core.
